decks.notes: The main concept of this Red Deck Wins aggro deck is to deploy low-cost, aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage to quickly deal damage to the opponent. Supporting cards like Burst Lightning, Lightning Strike, and Reckless Rage provide additional burn damage to help finish off the opponent. Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins can also provide extra sources of damage when needed. This deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with fast, efficient creatures and burn spells to secure a swift victory.

This UW Control deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage. It utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, along with board wipes like Supreme Verdict to control the battlefield. The deck also includes card draw and filtering options like Consult the Star Charts and Meticulous Archive to ensure consistent answers. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion provides additional value and flexibility. By controlling the game and out-valuing opponents, this deck aims to establish dominance and win through superior resource management.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the Greasefang and Parhelion II combo, using cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to generate overwhelming advantage and put pressure on the opponent. It includes support cards like Formidable Speaker and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect the combo pieces. The deck also includes removal and utility cards like Witherbloom Command and Skysovereign, Consul Flagship to deal with threats and gain additional value. The goal is to establish a dominant board presence and outpace the opponent with powerful combo interactions.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on early game aggression and maintaining board presence. It includes creatures like Archon of Emeria, Llanowar Elves, and Skyclave Apparition to quickly establish a strong board. The deck also utilizes disruptive cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Ouroboroid to hinder the opponent's strategy. With cards like Collected Company and Elspeth, Storm Slayer providing additional support and versatility, the deck aims to outpace and overwhelm the opponent before they can set up their own game plan.

This deck focuses on using Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that overwhelms the opponent. It includes cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize for control, as well as Monument to Endurance for protection. The deck also features various lands to support the combo, such as Godless Shrine and The Mycosynth Gardens. Ultimately, the goal is to set up the combo pieces and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victory in a 1 vs 1 matchup.

The deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field to generate a large amount of mana quickly and efficiently. By casting spells like Scapeshift and Spelunking, the goal is to ramp up mana production and combo off to achieve a powerful win condition. In combination with cards like Lumra, Bellow of the Woods and Arboreal Grazer, the deck aims to create a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent with a sudden burst of power. Additionally, cards like Boseiju, Who Endures and Thassa's Oracle provide additional utility and ways to close out the game.

The main focus of this aggro Red Deck Wins deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast, direct damage from creatures and burn spells. Utilizing efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, alongside powerful damage spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and close out the game quickly. With a mix of aggressive creatures, burn spells, and lands that can deal damage, this deck is designed to be proactive and aggressive in order to secure victory.

This aggressive Red Deck Wins Pioneer 1 vs 1 deck focuses on dealing damage quickly and efficiently. Cards like Burst Lightning, Monastery Swiftspear, and Screaming Nemesis help chip away at the opponent's life total, while Monstrous Rage and Reckless Rage provide additional damage and removal spells. The inclusion of Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins give the deck valuable land-based threats and reach. By prioritizing speed and aggression, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents before they have a chance to stabilize.

This deck focuses on using a combination of counterspells and removal spells to control the game, while also utilizing card draw and prowess creatures to maintain card advantage and pressure the opponent. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan, protect its own threats, and ultimately outvalue the opponent by efficiently trading resources. The deck aims to maintain control of the board and effectively close out the game before the opponent can establish a dominant position.

The deck focuses on fast-paced aggression with a combination of powerful creatures like Cori-Steel Cutter and Monastery Swiftspear, as well as direct damage spells like Burst Lightning and Torch the Tower. It also includes disruptive elements like Boomerang Basics and Den of the Bugbear to maintain control over the board. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly and efficiently with a mix of creature damage and burn spells, utilizing the UR color combination's strengths in both direct damage and creature aggression.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by bouncing opponent's permanents back to their hand, countering key spells, and drawing cards to maintain card advantage. It utilizes Izzet colors for a combination of red burn spells and blue card draw and counterspells. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while advancing its own game plan through card advantage and tempo swings. It is designed to be versatile and adaptable to various matchups, making it a strong contender in Pioneer 1 vs 1 matches.

This deck focuses on aggressive plays using low-cost creatures and burn spells to deal damage quickly to the opponent. It utilizes cards like Soul-Scar Mage, Emberheart Challenger, and Monastery Swiftspear to apply pressure early on, while also including cards like Reckless Rage and Burst Lightning to remove blockers or deal additional damage. The deck aims to take control of the board early and maintain pressure throughout the game, using efficient spells and creatures to quickly close out the match before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, utilizing cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to generate powerful synergies. It also incorporates control elements with cards like Thoughtseize and Witherbloom Command to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck aims to establish a dominant board presence and overwhelm the opponent with a combination of strong creatures and disruptive plays. It utilizes a mix of creatures, spells, and lands to create a well-rounded strategy that can adapt to different matchups.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy through a combination of hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress, removal spells such as Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, and resilient threats like Badgermole Cub and Sentinel of the Nameless City. The deck aims to grind out advantage over time, using mana dorks like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves to accelerate into powerful cards like Ouroboroid and Maelstrom Pulse. By utilizing a mixture of disruption, removal, and efficient creatures, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent.

The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive creature strategy, supported by cards like Archon of Emeria, Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, and Skyclave Apparition. The deck also includes ramp creatures such as Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves to accelerate the mana curve and allow for powerful plays like Collected Company. The inclusion of cards like Enduring Innocence and Elspeth, Storm Slayer provides additional removal and disruption to control the board, while still maintaining the aggressive pressure.

This deck focuses on controlling the board and disrupting the opponent's strategy through counterspells, removal, and card advantage. It utilizes cards like Dovin's Veto, Teferi, Hero of Dominaria, and Supreme Verdict to maintain control while also generating value with Yorion, Sky Nomad and Memory Deluge. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent by efficiently dealing with threats and drawing into powerful spells. By controlling the pace of the game and resource management, this deck looks to grind out victories through attrition and strategic decision-making.
